beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, toast, check crew messages on phone
beats.reasoning: Derek's routine: up before dawn, quiet house, early start mentality. 30 min is typical for his morning ritual before heading out.

beats.activity: Drive to Bellevue job site, listening to sports radio
beats.reasoning: Renton to Bellevue across I-405: 40 min typical. No transit strike today, smooth Friday morning drive expected.

beats.activity: Breakfast at Mel's Diner, crew messages, coffee refill
beats.reasoning: Derek's standing ritual before site work. Diner breakfast fuels the day and lets him catch up on crew comms.

beats.activity: Walk to job site, review inspection checklist
beats.reasoning: Short walk from diner to site. Friday morning, ready to work.

beats.activity: Framing and concrete inspections, crew coordination, equipment checks
beats.reasoning: Morning push: 4.5 hours of typical foreman work—inspections, measurements, crew briefings. Warm day but not extreme; good working conditions.

beats.activity: Lunch at Jimmy John's, sit in truck, check site logs
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch near site. Derek's no-nonsense style: eat fast, review documentation, back to work.

beats.activity: Afternoon inspections, final crew check-ins, wrap-up logs and safety review
beats.reasoning: Long afternoon block: 4.75 hours. Warm Friday, so Derek keeps crew hydrated and calls it before peak heat. Ends early enough to beat traffic and get home for family time.

beats.activity: Drive home from Bellevue to Renton, sports radio
beats.reasoning: Friday evening commute: 40 min expected. Leaving at 5:30 PM avoids rush-hour spike and gets Derek home by 6:10 PM.

beats.activity: Shower, change clothes, quick dinner with kids—grilling burgers
beats.reasoning: Derek cleans up from the site, spends time with family. Warm Friday evening perfect for backyard grilling. No baseball practice tonight (it's a Friday, season winds down).

beats.activity: Help kids with homework, sit on porch with a cold beer, watch the warm evening settle in
beats.reasoning: Family time, then wind-down. Derek's been energized this week; he's content at home. Pride events in Capitol Hill don't concern him; he's a Renton guy with family priorities.

beats.activity: Check crew messages, review tomorrow's schedule, tidy up kitchen
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down: Derek reviews work comms and prep for Saturday. No big plans tomorrow, so he's relaxed.

diary: Started the day before sunrise like always—coffee and toast while scrolling through crew messages. The drive to Bellevue was smooth, caught some sports talk on the radio. Hit Mel's for breakfast and a refill; that place never disappoints. The job site was solid today. Framing inspections went clean, concrete looked good. Spent most of the afternoon coordinating with the crew, making sure everything checked out for safety. Grabbed a Jimmy John's around lunch and ate in the truck, reviewing the logs. By the time I wrapped up and headed home, the whole city felt different—apparently Seattle Pride was kicking off downtown, streets packed and festive. I missed the chaos, stayed on the Bellevue side. Got home, fired up the grill for the kids, threw some burgers on. After dinner we worked through homework and I just sat on the porch with a cold beer, watching the evening cool down. Good day. Tired, but good.
thought_process: Today felt steady and uncomplicated. I focused on the job—inspections, crew coordination, keeping everything safe and on track. That's what matters to me at this stage. I heard about Pride happening downtown but didn't feel drawn to it; I've got my routine, my family waiting at home. The warm weather helped my mood; there's something about a good Friday that makes the week feel worth it. By evening, sitting with the beer and watching the kids settle down, I felt satisfied. Not every day needs to be eventful. Sometimes just executing the plan well and being present for the people at home is enough.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
